-- 3 of 22 --

THE PERSUASION QUADRANT
WANTS 	NEEDS
FEARS 	ACTIONS
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 22 --

THE “WANTS” BLOCK
‣ The first thing on your prospect’s mind 	most of
the time.
‣ 	The persuasion goals is to speak to 	top-of-mind.
‣ Think “urgent” rather than “important”.
‣ How to determine a want: 	“When my prospect
wakes up thinking this issue, what’s the first
thing that goes through their mind?”
‣ 	Hint: most prospects confuse “wants” with “needs”
in how they speak to themselves (not always)…
WANTS
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 22 --

THE “WANTS” BLOCK
‣ “I 	need 	to lose weight.”
‣ 	“I 	need 	a nice cup of coffee."
‣ “I 	want 	to have sex with my partner.”
‣ “I 	need 	to watch ‘The Orville’ tonight.”
‣ 	“I 	need 	a better smartphone."
‣ “I 	need 	to earn more money from copywriting.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 22 --

THE “WANTS” BLOCK
‣ Notice that all of those but one were 	wants, not needs.
‣ 	Even with the “I need to have sex”, the 	deep need 	is under the
surface (we’ll talk about that next.)
‣ How to create wants:
‣ “Would you like to ________?”
‣ 	“Wouldn’t it be fun to _______?”
‣ “You’re probably wanting to _______.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 22 --

THE “WANTS” BLOCK
‣ What you put in that blank needs to work 	practically 	in all three
examples.
‣ 	BAD Example: “protect yourself from sudden heart failure” sounds
okay in the first example, but downright silly in the second and third.
‣ However, someone who’s 	need 	is to protect against heart failure
(even here, there is a 	deep need 	of “ensure your family is taken care
of”) might have these for wants:
‣ “rest easier at night”, “climb stairs without panting or losing breath”,
“have a great checkup at your next doctor’s visit.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 22 --

THE “NEEDS” BLOCK
‣ The thing 	under the want.
‣ 	Often the last thing 	someone thinks about
unless they’re very tuned into themselves…until
it’s too late.
‣ Think “important” rather than “urgent”, and
usually connect to 	a hook.
‣ How to determine a need: 	“What does my
prospect dread the most?”
‣ 	Determining 	dreads 	help you determine needs, as
a need is 	the inverse.
NEEDS
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 22 --

THE “NEEDS” BLOCK
‣ “I 	dread 	the thought of a heart attack.”
‣ 	WANT: 	“Wouldn’t you 	like 	peace of mind when it comes to your
heart health?” (You don’t need to use the word “want”.)
‣ NEED: 	“You 	need 	to the latest blood test to make sure you don’t
have ‘The Heart Killer Gene’…” (That would never be top-of-mind)
‣ “I 	dread 	the thought of my partner leaving me.”
‣ 	WANT: 	“Want 	to spice up your love life?”
‣ NEED: 	“You 	need 	to learn the signals your wife is giving you.
Signals that she’s about to leave. That she’s secretly unhappy.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 22 --

THE “NEEDS” BLOCK
‣ “I 	want 	to lose weight.”
‣ 	“I 	need 	to live to see my kids grow up and not die early.”
‣ “I 	want 	to watch ‘The Orville’ tonight.”
‣ “I 	need 	more downtime. I’m stressed and if I don’t relax it will
kill me.”
‣ 	“I 	want 	to earn more money from copywriting.”
‣ “I 	need 	to feel safe, secure, and relevant…and I need to support
my family.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 22 --

THE “FEARS” BLOCK
‣ What drives 75% of human motivation.
‣ 	Dreads gave you the fears.
‣ Surface Fears are top-of-mind. Deep Fears are
where you need to venture as a copywriter (as
well as tapping on the surface. : )
‣ The best way to anchor fears is 	duality. 	Also
called “push/pull”.
‣ 	(Pull) “Want to spice up your love life?” (Push)
“Are you worried your partner may secretly be
bored…even considering cheating?”
FEARS
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 22 --

THE “FEARS” BLOCK
‣ Surface Fear: “I can’t lose weight no matter how hard I try.”
‣ 	DEEP FEARS: “I’m unlovable and undesirable. My partner may
leave me unless I do something about my weight. The way I
pant going upstairs, I fear having a heart attack.”
‣ Surface Fear: “I don’t know if I’ll be able to pay rent.”
‣ DEEP FEARS: “I believe I’m not worthy of wealth. Money is the
root of all evil. Rich people are dicks.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 22 --

THE “FEARS” BLOCK
‣ Fears can be more gently tapped by the Socratic Method (i.e. asking
questions with a predetermined outcome.)
‣ 	“Ever worry that those excess pounds or occasional indigestion
may in fact be a deadly heart attack just waiting to happen?”
‣ “Do you know for certain your wife is happy? Did you know that
73% of women surveyed confessed they never told their
partners they were unsatisfied sexually?”
‣ “Did you know that your gums are a hiding place for heart-
damaging bacteria? And that dental hygiene is directly connect
heart disease?” (I’ll come back to this one… : )
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 22 --

THE “ACTIONS” BLOCK
‣ An action is anything that 	demands motion
toward.
‣ 	Actions are usually 	nested. That means there’s
more than one motion in a group that someone
has to do.
‣ Actions are the easiest block to master.
ACTIONS
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 22 --

THE “ACTIONS” BLOCK
‣ Powerful CTAs: 	“Drop what you’re doing and go visit this page right
now…while (topic) is fresh on your mind.” (You’re actually wanting
them to 	buy, which is a set of motions.)
‣ 	Simple “do this” commands: 	“I want you to promise yourself
something right now: _______.”
‣ NLP commands: “Pay attention for the next few minutes”, “Imagine
for a moment _____.”, “Remember: _______.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 22 --

OVERLAYING THE QUADRANT
(First Name),
Did you know that your gums are a hiding place for heart-damaging bacteria? And that
proper dental hygiene can help keep your entire body more healthy?
Now that I have your attention, I’m like to remind you about your next appointment.
You’re overdue for your next cleaning.
I can relate – life gets in the way of what we know we need to do.
Just remember: your upcoming cleaning means more than a brighter smile. It can also
mean less harmful bacteria, as well as fresh breath for those crucial first impressions.
Click the link below now to book your appointment while this is fresh on your mind:
LINK
See you next week!
NAME
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 22 --

WORD ALCHEMY
‣ want ==> 	desire, crave, long for
‣ 	need ==> 	demand, deserve, insist upon
‣ fear ==> 	horrified, nearly died, panicking
‣ Read my sales letter. You’ll now be able to spot the 	empathy,
alchemy, and quadrants 	more easily (plus the NLP)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
